blocks.content: Julian arrived at the Carteret County station at five to ten.

It was a small building for a small jurisdiction — two deputies behind a front desk, a waiting area with four chairs, a coffee machine that looked like it had been there since the building was new. Davenport was waiting for him in a room just off the main hall. Hat on the table. Coffee in a styrofoam cup.
Julian sat. Davenport had a recorder on the table and a notepad and the look of a man who hadn't slept but had made peace with it.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Before we start — I spoke with the group this morning. They made the same argument you'd expect. Door locked from inside, window is a twenty-foot drop. They want me to close it as natural.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I know. I heard them talking.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: No. It's not wrong. It just starts from the wrong premise.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The locked room tells you nobody was in the room when he died. It doesn't tell you nobody did anything to him before he went into that room.
